We apply Stokes’ Theorem:  
\[
\int_C \vec{F} \cdot d\vec{r} = \iint_S (\nabla \times \vec{F}) \cdot \vec{n} \, dS.
\]  
Compute curl:  
\[
\nabla \times \vec{F} = 
\begin{vmatrix}
\vec{i} & \vec{j} & \vec{k} \\
\partial_x & \partial_y & \partial_z \\
z^2 & y^2 & x
\end{vmatrix}
= (0-0)\vec{i} - (1-2z)\vec{j} + (0-0)\vec{k} = (2z-1)\vec{j}.
\]  
The triangle lies on the plane \(x+y+z=1\), with upward normal \(\vec{n} = \frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}(1,1,1)\). Dot product:
